records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Travelling for Hajj and Other Acts of Obedience
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2388 - Ja'far ibn Bashir narrated from Ibrahim ibn al-Fadl, from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
"When Allah (swt), the Glorious and Majestic, decrees sustenance for a servant in a particular land, He creates a need for him in that land."
records.arabicText: 2388 - وَ رَوَى جَعْفَرُ بْنُ بَشِيرٍ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ اَلْفَضْلِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «إِذَا سَبَّبَ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لِعَبْدٍ اَلرِّزْقَ فِي أَرْضٍ جَعَلَ لَهُ فِيهَا حَاجَةً».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2388 - Ja'far ibn Bashir narrated from Ibrahim ibn al-Fadl, from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "When Allah (swt), the Glorious and Majestic, decrees sustenance for a servant in a particular land, He creates a need for him in that land."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Days and Times Recommended for Travel and Days and Times Disliked for Travel
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2389 - Hafs ibn Ghayath al-Nakha'i narrated from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as):
"Whoever intends to travel should travel on a Saturday, for if a stone were to be dislodged from a mountain on a Saturday, Allah (swt), the Glorious and Majestic, would return it to its place. And whoever finds difficulties in fulfilling their needs should seek them on a Tuesday, for it is the day on which Allah (swt), the Glorious and Majestic, softened iron for Dawood (David), peace be upon him."
records.arabicText: 2389 - رَوَى حَفْصُ بْنُ غِيَاثٍ اَلنَّخَعِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «مَنْ أَرَادَ سَفَراً فَلْيُسَافِرْ يَوْمَ اَلسَّبْتِ فَلَوْ أَنَّ حَجَراً زَالَ عَنْ جَبَلٍ فِي يَوْمِ اَلسَّبْتِ لَرَدَّهُ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ إِلَى مَكَانِهِ وَ مَنْ تَعَذَّرَتْ عَلَيْهِ اَلْحَوَائِجُ فَلْيَلْتَمِسْ طَلَبَهَا يَوْمَ اَلثَّلاَثَاءِ فَإِنَّهُ اَلْيَوْمُ اَلَّذِي أَلاَنَ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فِيهِ اَلْحَدِيدَ لِدَاوُدَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2389 - Hafs ibn Ghayath al-Nakha'i narrated from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever intends to travel should travel on a Saturday, for if a stone were to be dislodged from a mountain on a Saturday, Allah (swt), the Glorious and Majestic, would return it to its place. And whoever finds difficulties in fulfilling their needs should seek them on a Tuesday, for it is the day on which Allah (swt), the Glorious and Majestic, softened iron for Dawood (David), peace be upon him."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Days and Times Recommended for Travel and Days and Times Disliked for Travel
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2390 - Ibrahim ibn Abi Yahya al-Madani narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as): "There is no harm in setting out on a journey on the night of Friday."
records.arabicText: 2390 - وَ رَوَى إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ أَبِي يَحْيَى اَلْمَدِينِيُّ عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : «لاَ بَأْسَ بِالْخُرُوجِ فِي اَلسَّفَرِ لَيْلَةَ اَلْجُمُعَةِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2390 - Ibrahim ibn Abi Yahya al-Madani narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm in setting out on a journey on the night of Friday."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Days and Times Recommended for Travel and Days and Times Disliked for Travel
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2391 - Abdullah ibn Sulayman narrated from Imam Abu Ja'far (as):
"The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace be upon him and his family, used to travel on Thursdays."
records.arabicText: 2391 - وَ رَوَى عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «كَانَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ يُسَافِرُ يَوْمَ اَلْخَمِيسِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2391 - Abdullah ibn Sulayman narrated from Imam Abu Ja'far (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace be upon him and his family, used to travel on Thursdays."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Days and Times Recommended for Travel and Days and Times Disliked for Travel
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2392 - Imam (as) said: "Thursday is a day loved by Allah (swt), His Messenger, and His angels."
records.arabicText: 2392 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : « يَوْمُ اَلْخَمِيسِ يَوْمٌ يُحِبُّهُ اَللَّهُ وَ رَسُولُهُ وَ مَلاَئِكَتُهُ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Days and Times Recommended for Travel and Days and Times Disliked for Travel
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2393 - Some Baghdadis wrote to Imam al-Hassan al-Thani (as) asking about traveling on a Wednesday that does not rotate.
Imam (as) replied: "Whoever departs on a Wednesday that does not rotate, in opposition to those who believe in bad omens, will be protected from all harm, healed from every affliction, and Allah (swt), the Exalted, will fulfill his need."
records.arabicText: 2393 - وَ كَتَبَ بَعْضُ اَلْبَغْدَادِيِّينَ إِلَى أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ اَلثَّانِي عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : يَسْأَلُهُ عَنِ اَلْخُرُوجِ يَوْمَ اَلْأَرْبِعَاءِ لاَ يَدُورُ فَكَتَبَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ «مَنْ خَرَجَ يَوْمَ اَلْأَرْبِعَاءِ لاَ يَدُورُ خِلاَفاً عَلَى أَهْلِ اَلطِّيَرَةِ وُقِيَ مِنْ كُلِّ آفَةٍ وَ عُوفِيَ مِنْ كُلِّ عَاهَةٍ وَ قَضَى اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لَهُ حَاجَتَهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2393 - Some Baghdadis wrote to Imam al-Hassan al-Thani (as) asking about traveling on a Wednesday that does not rotate.
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever departs on a Wednesday that does not rotate, in opposition to those who believe in bad omens, will be protected from all harm, healed from every affliction, and Allah (swt), the Exalted, will fulfill his need."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Days and Times Recommended for Travel and Days and Times Disliked for Travel
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2394 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said:
"Travel by night, for the earth is folded during the night."
records.arabicText: 2394 - وَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : «عَلَيْكُمْ بِالسَّيْرِ بِاللَّيْلِ فَإِنَّ اَلْأَرْضَ تُطْوَى بِاللَّيْلِ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2394 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Travel by night, for the earth is folded during the night."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Days and Times Recommended for Travel and Days and Times Disliked for Travel
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2395 - In a narration by Jamil ibn Darraj and Hammad ibn Uthman from Abu Abdullah (as) he said: "The earth is folded at the end of the night."
records.arabicText: 2395 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ جَمِيلِ بْنِ دَرَّاجٍ وَ حَمَّادِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «اَلْأَرْضُ تُطْوَى مِنْ آخِرِ اَللَّيْلِ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2395 - In a narration by Jamil ibn Darraj and Hammad ibn Uthman from Abu Abdullah (as) he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The earth is folded at the end of the night."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Days and Times Recommended for Travel and Days and Times Disliked for Travel
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2396 - Muhammad ibn Yahya Al-Khath'ami narrated from him, peace be upon him:
"Do not go out on Friday for any need, but if it is Saturday and the sun has risen, then go out for your need."
records.arabicText: 2396 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى اَلْخَثْعَمِيُّ عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «لاَ تَخْرُجْ يَوْمَ اَلْجُمُعَةِ فِي حَاجَةٍ فَإِذَا كَانَ يَوْمُ اَلسَّبْتِ وَ طَلَعَتِ اَلشَّمْسُ فَاخْرُجْ فِي حَاجَتِكَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2396 - Muhammad ibn Yahya Al-Khath'ami narrated from him, peace be upon him:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Do not go out on Friday for any need, but if it is Saturday and the sun has risen, then go out for your need."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Days and Times Recommended for Travel and Days and Times Disliked for Travel
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2397 - Abu Ayyub Al-Khazzaz and Abdullah ibn Sinan asked Abu Abdullah (as) about the saying of Allah (swt), the Exalted:
"So when the prayer has been concluded, disperse within the land and seek from the bounty of Allah (swt)." (Surah Al-Jumu'ah 62:10)
Imam (as) said: "The prayer refers to the day of Friday, and the dispersal refers to the day of Saturday."
records.arabicText: 2397 - وَ سَأَلَ أَبُو أَيُّوبَ اَلْخَزَّازُ وَ عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ سِنَانٍ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ قَوْلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ «فَإِذٰا قُضِيَتِ اَلصَّلاٰةُ فَانْتَشِرُوا فِي اَلْأَرْضِ وَ اِبْتَغُوا مِنْ فَضْلِ اَللّٰهِ» فَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ «اَلصَّلاَةُ يَوْمَ اَلْجُمُعَةِ وَ اَلاِنْتِشَارُ يَوْمَ اَلسَّبْتِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2397 - Abu Ayyub Al-Khazzaz and Abdullah ibn Sinan asked Abu Abdullah (as) about the saying of Allah (swt), the Exalted:
"So when the prayer has been concluded, disperse within the land and seek from the bounty of Allah (swt)." (Surah Al-Jumu'ah 62:10)
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The prayer refers to the day of Friday, and the dispersal refers to the day of Saturday."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Days and Times Recommended for Travel and Days and Times Disliked for Travel
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2398 - Imam (as) said: "Saturday is for us, and Sunday is for Banu Umayyah."
records.arabicText: 2398 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : « اَلسَّبْتُ لَنَا وَ اَلْأَحَدُ لِبَنِي أُمَيَّةَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2398 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Saturday is for us, and Sunday is for Banu Umayyah."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Days and Times Recommended for Travel and Days and Times Disliked for Travel
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2399 - Imam (as) said: "Do not travel on Monday, nor seek any need on that day."
records.arabicText: 2399 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «لاَ تُسَافِرْ يَوْمَ اَلْإِثْنَيْنِ وَ لاَ تَطْلُبْ فِيهِ حَاجَةً».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Days and Times Recommended for Travel and Days and Times Disliked for Travel
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2400 - It is narrated from Abu Ayyub Al-Khazzaz that he said:
"We intended to leave, so we went to bid farewell to Abu Abdullah (as).
Imam (as) said: 'It seems you are seeking the blessing of Monday.'
We said: 'Yes.'
Imam (as) said: 'What day is more ominous than Monday? On that day, we lost our Prophet (sw), may Allah (swt) bless him and his family, and revelation ceased to descend upon us. Do not depart on Monday; instead, depart on Tuesday.'"
records.arabicText: 2400 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ أَبِي أَيُّوبَ اَلْخَزَّازِ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : أَرَدْنَا أَنْ نَخْرُجَ فَجِئْنَا نُسَلِّمُ عَلَى أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَقَالَ «كَأَنَّكُمْ طَلَبْتُمْ بَرَكَةَ اَلْإِثْنَيْنِ » قُلْنَا نَعَمْ قَالَ «فَأَيُّ يَوْمٍ أَعْظَمُ شُؤْماً مِنْ يَوْمِ اَلْإِثْنَيْنِ فَقَدْنَا فِيهِ نَبِيَّنَا صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ وَ اِرْتَفَعَ اَلْوَحْيُ عَنَّا لاَ تَخْرُجُوا يَوْمَ اَلْإِثْنَيْنِ وَ اُخْرُجُوا يَوْمَ اَلثَّلاَثَاءِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2400 - It is narrated from Abu Ayyub Al-Khazzaz that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"We intended to leave, so we went to bid farewell to Abu Abdullah (as).
Imam (as) said: 'It seems you are seeking the blessing of Monday.'
We said: 'Yes.'
Imam (as) said: 'What day is more ominous than Monday? On that day, we lost our Prophet (sw), may Allah (swt) bless him and his family, and revelation ceased to descend upon us. Do not depart on Monday; instead, depart on Tuesday.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Days and Times Recommended for Travel and Days and Times Disliked for Travel
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2401 - It is narrated by Muhammad ibn Humran from his father, from Abu Abdullah (as):
"Whoever travels or marries while the moon is in Scorpio will not see goodness."
records.arabicText: 2401 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ حُمْرَانَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «مَنْ سَافَرَ أَوْ تَزَوَّجَ وَ اَلْقَمَرُ فِي اَلْعَقْرَبِ لَمْ يَرَ اَلْحُسْنَى.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2401 - It is narrated by Muhammad ibn Humran from his father, from Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever travels or marries while the moon is in Scorpio will not see goodness."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Days and Times Recommended for Travel and Days and Times Disliked for Travel
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2402 - It is narrated from Abdul Malik ibn A'yan who said:
"I said to Abu Abdullah (as) 'I have been tested with this knowledge (of astrology). When I intend to fulfill a need and observe the rising star, if I see it as unfavorable, I sit and do not proceed. But if I see it as favorable, I proceed with my need.'
Imam (as) said to me: 'Do you accomplish it?'
I said: 'Yes.'
Imam (as) said: 'Burn your books.'"
records.arabicText: 2402 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَلْمَلِكِ بْنِ أَعْيَنَ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ إِنِّي قَدِ اُبْتُلِيتُ بِهَذَا اَلْعِلْمِ فَأُرِيدُ اَلْحَاجَةَ فَإِذَا نَظَرْتُ إِلَى اَلطَّالِعِ وَ رَأَيْتُ اَلطَّالِعَ اَلشَّرَّ جَلَسْتُ وَ لَمْ أَذْهَبْ فِيهَا وَ إِذَا رَأَيْتُ اَلطَّالِعَ اَلْخَيْرَ ذَهَبْتُ فِي اَلْحَاجَةِ فَقَالَ لِي «تَقْضِي » قُلْتُ نَعَمْ قَالَ «أَحْرِقْ كُتُبَكَ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2402 - It is narrated from Abdul Malik ibn A'yan who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I said to Abu Abdullah (as) 'I have been tested with this knowledge (of astrology). When I intend to fulfill a need and observe the rising star, if I see it as unfavorable, I sit and do not proceed. But if I see it as favorable, I proceed with my need.'
Imam (as) said to me: 'Do you accomplish it?'
I said: 'Yes.'
Imam (as) said: 'Burn your books.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Days and Times Recommended for Travel and Days and Times Disliked for Travel
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2403 - It is narrated by Sulaiman ibn Ja'far al-Ja'fari from Abu al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as):
"Ill omen for a traveler on his journey lies in six things:
A croaking crow on his right side,
A dog wagging its tail,
A howling wolf that howls facing the man, sitting on its tail, howling and then raising and lowering itself three times,
A deer crossing from the right to the left,
A screeching owl,
And an old gray-haired woman exposing herself, or a mutilated she-donkey, meaning one with clipped ears.
Whoever feels apprehension in themselves from any of these, let them say: 'I seek refuge with You, my Lord (azj), from the evil that I sense within myself; so protect me from it.'
Imam (as) said: 'They will then be protected from it.'"
records.arabicText: 2403 - وَ رَوَى سُلَيْمَانُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ اَلْجَعْفَرِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ مُوسَى بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «اَلشُّؤْمُ لِلْمُسَافِرِ فِي طَرِيقِهِ فِي سِتَّةٍ اَلْغُرَابِ اَلنَّاعِقِ عَنْ يَمِينِهِ وَ اَلْكَلْبِ اَلنَّاشِرِ لِذَنَبِهِ وَ اَلذِّئْبِ اَلْعَاوِي اَلَّذِي يَعْوِي فِي وَجْهِ اَلرَّجُلِ وَ هُوَ مُقْعٍ عَلَى ذَنَبِهِ يَعْوِي ثُمَّ يَرْتَفِعُ ثُمَّ يَنْخَفِضُ ثَلاَثاً وَ اَلظَّبْيِ اَلسَّانِحِ مِنْ يَمِينٍ إِلَى شِمَالٍ وَ اَلْبُومَةِ اَلصَّارِخَةِ وَ اَلْمَرْأَةِ اَلشَّمْطَاءِ تَلْقَى فَرْجَهَا وَ اَلْأَتَانِ اَلْعَضْبَاءِ يَعْنِي اَلْجَدْعَاءَ فَمَنْ أَوْجَسَ فِي نَفْسِهِ مِنْهُنَّ شَيْئاً فَلْيَقُلِ اِعْتَصَمْتُ بِكَ يَا رَبِّ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا أَجِدُ فِي نَفْسِي فَاعْصِمْنِي مِنْ ذَلِكَ قَالَ فَيُعْصَمُ مِنْ ذَلِكَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2403 - It is narrated by Sulaiman ibn Ja'far al-Ja'fari from Abu al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Ill omen for a traveler on his journey lies in six things:
A croaking crow on his right side,
A dog wagging its tail,
A howling wolf that howls facing the man, sitting on its tail, howling and then raising and lowering itself three times,
A deer crossing from the right to the left,
A screeching owl,
And an old gray-haired woman exposing herself, or a mutilated she-donkey, meaning one with clipped ears.
Whoever feels apprehension in themselves from any of these, let them say: 'I seek refuge with You, my Lord (azj), from the evil that I sense within myself; so protect me from it.'
Imam (as) said: 'They will then be protected from it.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Beginning a Journey with Charity
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2404 - It is narrated by Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub from Abdul Rahman ibn Al-Hajjaj that Abu Abdullah (as) said: "Give charity and then set out on your journey on any day you wish."
records.arabicText: 2404 - رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ اَلْحَجَّاجِ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «تَصَدَّقْ وَ اُخْرُجْ أَيَّ يَوْمٍ شِئْتَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2404 - It is narrated by Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub from Abdul Rahman ibn Al-Hajjaj that Abu Abdullah (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Give charity and then set out on your journey on any day you wish."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Beginning a Journey with Charity
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2405 - It is narrated from Hammad ibn Uthman who said: I asked Abu Abdullah (as) whether it is disliked to travel on certain days considered unfavorable, such as Wednesdays and others.
Imam (as) replied: "Begin your journey with charity, set out whenever it seems appropriate to you, recite Ayat al-Kursi, and perform cupping (hijama) if you feel the need."
records.arabicText: 2405 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَ يُكْرَهُ اَلسَّفَرُ فِي شَيْءٍ مِنَ اَلْأَيَّامِ اَلْمَكْرُوهَةِ مِثْلِ اَلْأَرْبِعَاءِ وَ غَيْرِهِ فَقَالَ «اِفْتَتِحْ سَفَرَكَ بِالصَّدَقَةِ وَ اُخْرُجْ إِذَا بَدَا لَكَ وَ اِقْرَأْ آيَةَ اَلْكُرْسِيِّ وَ اِحْتَجِمْ إِذَا بَدَا لَكَ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2405 - It is narrated from Hammad ibn Uthman who said: I asked Abu Abdullah (as) whether it is disliked to travel on certain days considered unfavorable, such as Wednesdays and others.
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Begin your journey with charity, set out whenever it seems appropriate to you, recite Ayat al-Kursi, and perform cupping (hijama) if you feel the need."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Beginning a Journey with Charity
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2406 - It is narrated from Ibn Abi Umair who said: I used to observe the stars and understand their movements and their rise, and from this, certain thoughts would enter my mind. I complained about this to Abu al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim, peace be upon them both.
Imam (as) said: "If something enters your mind, give charity to the first needy person you encounter, and then proceed, for Allah (swt), the Exalted and Glorious, will ward off harm from you."
records.arabicText: 2406 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ اِبْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : كُنْتُ أَنْظُرُ فِي اَلنُّجُومِ وَ أَعْرِفُهَا وَ أَعْرِفُ اَلطَّالِعَ فَيَدْخُلُنِي مِنْ ذَلِكَ شَيْءٌ فَشَكَوْتُ ذَلِكَ إِلَى أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ مُوسَى بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ فَقَالَ «إِذَا وَقَعَ فِي نَفْسِكَ شَيْءٌ فَتَصَدَّقْ عَلَى أَوَّلِ مِسْكِينٍ ثُمَّ اِمْضِ فَإِنَّ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ يَدْفَعُ عَنْكَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2406 - It is narrated from Ibn Abi Umair who said: I used to observe the stars and understand their movements and their rise, and from this, certain thoughts would enter my mind. I complained about this to Abu al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim, peace be upon them both.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "If something enters your mind, give charity to the first needy person you encounter, and then proceed, for Allah (swt), the Exalted and Glorious, will ward off harm from you."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Beginning a Journey with Charity
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2407 - It is narrated from Kirdin, who reported from Abu Abdullah (as):
"Whoever gives charity in the morning, Allah (swt), the Exalted and Glorious, will avert the misfortune of that day from him."
records.arabicText: 2407 - وَ رَوَى كِرْدِينٌ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «مَنْ تَصَدَّقَ بِصَدَقَةٍ إِذَا أَصْبَحَ دَفَعَ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ عَنْهُ نَحْسَ ذَلِكَ اَلْيَوْمِ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2407 - It is narrated from Kirdin, who reported from Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever gives charity in the morning, Allah (swt), the Exalted and Glorious, will avert the misfortune of that day from him."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Beginning a Journey with Charity
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2408 - It is narrated by Harun ibn Kharejah from Muhammad ibn Muslim, who reported from Abu Ja'far, peace be upon him:
"Ali ibn al-Husayn, peace be upon them both, whenever he intended to travel to one of his properties, would purchase safety from Allah (swt), the Exalted, with whatever was available to him. This would be done as he placed his foot in the stirrup. If Allah (swt), the Exalted, safeguarded him and he returned safely, he would praise and thank Allah (swt), the Exalted, and give charity with whatever was available to him."
records.arabicText: 2408 - وَ رَوَى هَارُونُ بْنُ خَارِجَةَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «كَانَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ اَلْحُسَيْنِ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ إِذَا أَرَادَ اَلْخُرُوجَ إِلَى بَعْضِ أَمْوَالِهِ اِشْتَرَى اَلسَّلاَمَةَ مِنَ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ بِمَا تَيَسَّرَ لَهُ وَ يَكُونُ ذَلِكَ إِذَا وَضَعَ رِجْلَهُ فِي اَلرِّكَابِ فَإِذَا سَلَّمَهُ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَ اِنْصَرَفَ حَمِدَ اَللَّهَ تَعَالَى وَ شَكَرَهُ وَ تَصَدَّقَ بِمَا تَيَسَّرَ لَهُ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2408 - It is narrated by Harun ibn Kharejah from Muhammad ibn Muslim, who reported from Abu Ja'far, peace be upon him:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Ali ibn al-Husayn, peace be upon them both, whenever he intended to travel to one of his properties, would purchase safety from Allah (swt), the Exalted, with whatever was available to him. This would be done as he placed his foot in the stirrup. If Allah (swt), the Exalted, safeguarded him and he returned safely, he would praise and thank Allah (swt), the Exalted, and give charity with whatever was available to him."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Supplications Recommended for the Traveler Upon Departure
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2414 - Musa ibn al-Qasim al-Bajali narrated from Sabbah al-Hadhdha’, who said:
I heard Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) say:
"If one of you, when intending to travel, stands at the door of their house, facing the direction they are heading towards, and recites Surah Al-Fatiha in front of them, to their right, and to their left, and recites Ayat al-Kursi in front of them, to their right, and to their left, and then says:
'O Allah (swt), protect me and protect what is with me. Grant me safety and grant safety to what is with me. Deliver me and deliver what is with me with Your good deliverance,'
Allah (swt) will protect them, protect what is with them, grant them safety, grant safety to what is with them, and deliver them and deliver what is with them."
Imam (as) then added: "O Sabbah, have you not seen someone being protected while what is with them is not protected, or being granted safety while what is with them is not granted safety, or reaching their destination while what is with them does not?"
I said: "Indeed, may I be your ransom."
records.arabicText: 2414 - رَوَى مُوسَى بْنُ اَلْقَاسِمِ اَلْبَجَلِيُّ عَنْ صَبَّاحٍ اَلْحَذَّاءِ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ مُوسَى بْنَ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقُولُ : «لَوْ كَانَ اَلرَّجُلُ مِنْكُمْ إِذَا أَرَادَ سَفَراً أَقَامَ عَلَى بَابِ دَارِهِ تِلْقَاءَ اَلْوَجْهِ اَلَّذِي يَتَوَجَّهُ إِلَيْهِ فَقَرَأَ فَاتِحَةَ اَلْكِتَابِ أَمَامَهُ وَ عَنْ يَمِينِهِ وَ عَنْ شِمَالِهِ وَ آيَةَ اَلْكُرْسِيِّ أَمَامَهُ وَ عَنْ يَمِينِهِ وَ عَنْ شِمَالِهِ ثُمَّ قَالَ اَللَّهُمَّ اِحْفَظْنِي وَ اِحْفَظْ مَا مَعِي وَ سَلِّمْنِي وَ سَلِّمْ مَا مَعِي وَ بَلِّغْنِي وَ بَلِّغْ مَا مَعِي بِبَلاَغِكَ اَلْحَسَنِ لَحَفِظَهُ اَللَّهُ وَ لَحَفِظَ مَا مَعَهُ وَ سَلَّمَهُ اَللَّهُ وَ سَلَّمَ مَا مَعَهُ وَ بَلَّغَهُ اَللَّهُ وَ بَلَّغَ مَا مَعَهُ » قَالَ ثُمَّ قَالَ «يَا صَبَّاحُ أَ مَا رَأَيْتَ اَلرَّجُلَ يُحْفَظُ وَ لاَ يُحْفَظُ مَا مَعَهُ وَ يَسْلَمُ وَ لاَ يَسْلَمُ مَا مَعَهُ وَ يَبْلُغُ وَ لاَ يَبْلُغُ مَا مَعَهُ» قُلْتُ بَلَى جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2414 - Musa ibn al-Qasim al-Bajali narrated from Sabbah al-Hadhdha’, who said:
I heard Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) say:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"If one of you, when intending to travel, stands at the door of their house, facing the direction they are heading towards, and recites Surah Al-Fatiha in front of them, to their right, and to their left, and recites Ayat al-Kursi in front of them, to their right, and to their left, and then says:
'O Allah (swt), protect me and protect what is with me. Grant me safety and grant safety to what is with me. Deliver me and deliver what is with me with Your good deliverance,'
Allah (swt) will protect them, protect what is with them, grant them safety, grant safety to what is with them, and deliver them and deliver what is with them."
Imam (as) then added: "O Sabbah, have you not seen someone being protected while what is with them is not protected, or being granted safety while what is with them is not granted safety, or reaching their destination while what is with them does not?"
I said: "Indeed, may I be your ransom."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Supplications Recommended for the Traveler Upon Departure
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2415 - When Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) intended to travel, he would say:
"O Allah (swt), make our path clear, improve our journey, and grant us immense well-being."
records.arabicText: 2415 - وَ كَانَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ إِذَا أَرَادَ سَفَراً قَالَ -: « اَللَّهُمَّ خَلِّ سَبِيلَنَا وَ أَحْسِنْ تَسْيِيرَنَا وَ أَعْظِمْ عَافِيَتَنَا ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2415 -
records.thaqalaynMatn:
When Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) intended to travel, he would say:
"O Allah (swt), make our path clear, improve our journey, and grant us immense well-being."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Supplications Recommended for the Traveler Upon Departure
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2416 - Ali ibn Asbat narrated from Imam Abu al-Hasan al-Ridha (as), who said:
Imam (as) said to me: "When you leave your house, whether for travel or otherwise, say:
'In the name of Allah (swt), I believe in Allah (swt), I rely upon Allah (swt). Whatever Allah (swt) wills happens. There is no power and no strength except with Allah (swt).'
The devils will then confront you, but the angels will strike their faces and say:
'What path do you have over someone who has mentioned Allah (swt), believed in Him, relied upon Him, and said,
‘Whatever Allah (swt) wills happens. There is no power and no strength except with Allah (swt)?'"
records.arabicText: 2416 - وَ رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَسْبَاطٍ عَنْ أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ اَلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ قَالَ لِي : «إِذَا خَرَجْتَ مِنْ مَنْزِلِكَ فِي سَفَرٍ أَوْ حَضَرٍ فَقُلْ: بِسْمِ اَللَّهِ آمَنْتُ بِاللَّهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ عَلَى اَللَّهِ مَا شَاءَ اَللَّهُ وَ لاَ حَوْلَ وَ لاَ قُوَّةَ إِلاَّ بِاللَّهِ فَتَلَقَّاهُ اَلشَّيَاطِينُ فَتَضْرِبُ اَلْمَلاَئِكَةُ وُجُوهَهَا وَ تَقُولُ مَا سَبِيلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ وَ قَدْ سَمَّى اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَ آمَنَ بِهِ وَ تَوَكَّلَ عَلَى اَللَّهِ وَ قَالَ مَا شَاءَ اَللَّهُ لاَ حَوْلَ وَ لاَ قُوَّةَ إِلاَّ بِاللَّهِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2416 - Ali ibn Asbat narrated from Imam Abu al-Hasan al-Ridha (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Imam (as) said to me: "When you leave your house, whether for travel or otherwise, say:
'In the name of Allah (swt), I believe in Allah (swt), I rely upon Allah (swt). Whatever Allah (swt) wills happens. There is no power and no strength except with Allah (swt).'
The devils will then confront you, but the angels will strike their faces and say:
'What path do you have over someone who has mentioned Allah (swt), believed in Him, relied upon Him, and said,
‘Whatever Allah (swt) wills happens. There is no power and no strength except with Allah (swt)?'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Supplications Recommended for the Traveler Upon Departure
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2417 - Abu Basir narrated from Abu Ja‘far (as), who said:
"Whoever says upon leaving the door of their house:
'I seek refuge in Allah (swt) from what the angels of Allah (swt) sought refuge from— from the evil of this day, from the evil of the devils, from the evil of those who oppose the friends of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Glorious, from the evil of jinn and humans, from the evil of wild beasts and harmful creatures, and from the evil of committing all forbidden acts. I entrust myself to Allah (swt) from every evil,'
Allah (swt) will forgive them, accept their repentance, suffice them in their concerns, prevent harm from reaching them, and protect them from evil."
records.arabicText: 2417 - وَ رَوَى أَبُو بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «مَنْ قَالَ حِينَ يَخْرُجُ مِنْ بَابِ دَارِهِ أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ مِمَّا عَاذَتْ مِنْهُ مَلاَئِكَةُ اَللَّهِ مِنْ شَرِّ هَذَا اَلْيَوْمِ وَ مِنْ شَرِّ اَلشَّيَاطِينِ وَ مِنْ شَرِّ مَنْ نَصَبَ لِأَوْلِيَاءِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَ مِنْ شَرِّ اَلْجِنِّ وَ اَلْإِنْسِ وَ مِنْ شَرِّ اَلسِّبَاعِ وَ اَلْهَوَامِّ وَ مِنْ شَرِّ رُكُوبِ اَلْمَحَارِمِ كُلِّهَا أُجِيرُ نَفْسِي بِاللَّهِ مِنْ كُلِّ شَرٍّ غَفَرَ اَللَّهُ لَهُ وَ تَابَ عَلَيْهِ وَ كَفَاهُ اَلْمُهِمَّ وَ حَجَزَهُ عَنِ اَلسُّوءِ وَ عَصَمَهُ مِنَ اَلشَّرِّ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2417 - Abu Basir narrated from Abu Ja‘far (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Whoever says upon leaving the door of their house:
'I seek refuge in Allah (swt) from what the angels of Allah (swt) sought refuge from— from the evil of this day, from the evil of the devils, from the evil of those who oppose the friends of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Glorious, from the evil of jinn and humans, from the evil of wild beasts and harmful creatures, and from the evil of committing all forbidden acts. I entrust myself to Allah (swt) from every evil,'
Allah (swt) will forgive them, accept their repentance, suffice them in their concerns, prevent harm from reaching them, and protect them from evil."
